description: Tangible is a platform to facilitate the structuring, modeling, and management of asset-backed financial transactions.

build_patterns.name: Agentic Architectures
evidence: responsible for pushing the boundaries of agentic computing and AI-first product development
constantly optimising coding agents and his life through open source experiments and custom apps
Data Scientist responsible for building the agentic models and simulations that de-risk the future of hardware finance
architecting the infrastructure that allows hardware founders to scale
build_patterns.description: Explicit references to 'agentic computing', 'coding agents', and 'agentic models and simulations' indicate they deploy autonomous agents/orchestrators that use tools, perform multi-step workflows, and simulate financing scenarios. Likely used for automating diligence, deal orchestration, and multi-step financial modeling.

build_patterns.name: Knowledge Graphs
evidence: Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time
collateral, receivables, and diligence
connects hardware founders with scalable capital
granular access controls mean your data stays exactly where you put it
matching algorithms that align ambitious hard-tech projects with the right capital
build_patterns.description: The product focuses on mapping assets, covenants, receivables, and parties into a single live data infrastructure. This indicates a structured entity/relationship layer (graph-like) with permissioning and RBI/RBAC controls to support fine-grained access and matchmaking between projects and lenders.

build_patterns.name: Micro-model Meshes
evidence: Our AI Products are provided through third-party service providers, including Anthropic and Google Cloud AI
Smart automation extracts collateral details, reconciles receivables, and highlights red flags
AI Products: Machine learning models, AI automation, AI predictive analytics
specialised roles: Principal AI Engineer, Data Scientist building agentic models and simulations
build_patterns.description: Multiple specialized model capabilities are described (extraction, reconciliation, red-flag detection, predictive analytics) and use of multiple AI providers suggests a mesh of smaller, task-specific models or model routing rather than a single monolithic model.

build_patterns.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
evidence: turn static data into the machine-readable knowledge needed to scale hard-tech
no hallucinations, no made-up numbers, just reliable reporting you can trust
live data infrastructure... Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time
AI powered diligence layer for transactions
build_patterns.description: Emphasis on grounding outputs in live reconciled asset data and explicit insistence on avoiding hallucinations strongly imply retrieval/grounding mechanisms (e.g., vector stores, document retrieval, canonical data sources) used alongside generative models to produce validated outputs.

build_patterns.name: Guardrail-as-LLM
evidence: no hallucinations, no made-up numbers, just reliable reporting you can trust
Serious security processes
You must not use the AI Products in any way that violates the terms or policies of any AI Service Provider
We translate what borrowers need into terms you can underwrite. Fewer calls, faster decisions. Our experts help implement risk-mitigating policies
build_patterns.description: Claims around preventing hallucinations, enforcing compliance/policies, and integrating expert risk controls indicate secondary validation layers or monitoring models that check/rectify model outputs for safety, accuracy, and regulatory compliance.

build_patterns.name: Vertical Data Moats
evidence: We build the financial infrastructure that lets hardware companies structure and manage debt funding
creating the standardized playbooks for financing new esoteric assets - starting with batteries
building the data science that makes hardware tech financeable
turning complex equipment performance into explainable, financeable data for capital markets
build_patterns.description: Strong domain focus (hard-tech, batteries, physical assets) and claims about turning specialized asset performance into financeable signals indicate the cultivation of proprietary, industry-specific datasets and models that are a defensible competitive advantage.

build_patterns.name: Continuous-learning Flywheels
evidence: Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time
We translate what borrowers need into terms you can underwrite
constantly optimising coding agents and his life through open source experiments and custom apps
Keep everything accurate and up-to-date automatically
build_patterns.description: Live reconciliation, automated updates, and references to continual optimisation suggest feedback loops where operational and transaction data are used to improve models and tooling over time, though an explicit training/feedback pipeline is not described in detail.

build_patterns.name: Natural-Language-to-Code
evidence: Covenants, waterfalls, triggers, defined terms, all mapped out
Build the reports your lenders actually need, structured the way you want them
We translate what borrowers need into terms you can underwrite
minutes to a personalised roadmap
build_patterns.description: The product exposes structured finance constructs (covenants, triggers, waterfalls) and promises personalised roadmaps and report-building; this suggests potential NL→specification/DSL translation for rules and reports, but direct evidence of an NL-to-code interface or automated rule generator is limited.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Cloud-hosted third-party LLM consumption (Anthropic + Google Cloud AI)
evidence: "We provide the AI Products through third-party service providers ('AI Service Providers'), including Anthropic and Google Cloud AI."
"your input, output, and personal information will be shared with and processed by these AI Service Providers"
discovered_patterns.description: Tangible delegates core model inference to commercial cloud-hosted LLM providers (explicitly Anthropic and Google Cloud AI). The product invokes external LLM APIs rather than running open-source models in-house.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: This is a widely used enterprise pattern: leveraging managed, high-quality LLM APIs to accelerate product delivery while outsourcing model maintenance and scaling.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Agentic / multi-agent orchestration for transaction workflows
evidence: "Principal AI Engineer, responsible for pushing the boundaries of agentic computing and AI-first product development."
"Data Scientist ... building the agentic models and simulations that de-risk the future of hardware finance."
"chains of interpretability"
discovered_patterns.description: They are building agentic systems and simulations — likely multi-agent orchestrations where specialized agents (e.g., data extractor, reconciler, risk assessor, rapport generator) collaborate/compose to automate diligence, reconciliation, and matchmaking workflows.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: Applying agentic multi-agent simulations to structured finance and real-world collateral assessment (hard-tech assets) is relatively uncommon and requires integrating domain-specific rules, simulations and interpretability chains.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Live data ingestion + machine-readable knowledge layer for asset-backed finance
evidence: "live data infrastructure. Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time."
"laying the AI foundations that turn static data into the machine-readable knowledge needed to scale hard-tech."
"Tangible to your data stack in minutes and keeps everything accurate and up-to-date automatically."
"Smart automation extracts collateral details, reconciles receivables, and highlights red flags"
discovered_patterns.description: They implement connectors/ETL to on‑prem/cloud data sources to ingest asset, receivable and collateral data into a canonical, machine-readable schema. This layer powers extraction, near-real-time reconciliation, monitoring, and downstream AI agents.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: The focus on a live, reconciled, canonical asset data layer tailored to esoteric hardware assets (e.g., batteries) is a strong vertical data engineering play that enables reliable AI outputs in a high-stakes finance domain.

evidence: "no hallucinations, no made-up numbers, just reliable reporting you can trust."
"chains of interpretability to turn complex physical data into clear insights."
"turning complex equipment performance into explainable, financeable data for capital markets."
discovered_patterns.description: They prioritise explainable outputs and build interpretability pipelines ('chains of interpretability') to convert raw physical/financial signals into auditable insights. This implies evaluation metrics beyond surface-level accuracy — e.g., traceability of claims, numeric reconciliation checks and domain-specific correctness tests.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: Explicitly engineering for auditable numeric fidelity (to avoid hallucinations) is critical in finance and less emphasized in consumer AI products — here it shapes model selection, prompting, and post-processing.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Cloud-first, connector-driven SaaS with enterprise security posture
evidence: "ISO 27001 certified infrastructure, end-to-end encryption, and granular access controls"
"Tangible to your data stack in minutes"
"We may share your data with third-party vendors... Cloud Computing Services... Data Storage Service Providers"
discovered_patterns.description: Tangible operates a cloud SaaS platform exposing data connectors and a live reconciliation engine, built on cloud compute and storage, backed by ISO27001, encryption, and access control. They integrate with customers' data stacks quickly (presumably via connectors, API/webhooks, or data pipelines).
discovered_patterns.why_notable: Strong enterprise security and quick connector onboarding are standard for fintech SaaS; notable here because of the sensitivity and complexity of asset-backed finance data.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Data governance + vendor data flow transparency
evidence: "your input, output, and personal information will be shared with and processed by these AI Service Providers"
"We are responsible for making decisions about how your personal information is processed."
"Data Protection Officer..."
"end-to-end encryption, and granular access controls"
discovered_patterns.description: Tangible documents explicit data flows to AI providers, retains a DPO, and enforces ISO27001-level controls. They emphasize transparency about which third parties see PII and formal contractual safeguards for vendors.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: Clear, explicit disclosure of AI vendor data handling increases trust in regulated finance contexts; it's an increasingly common but important compliance-first pattern.

discovered_patterns.pattern_name: Verticalized proprietary data play for hardware-backed finance (batteries focus)
evidence: "creating the standardized playbooks for financing new esoteric assets - starting with batteries"
"turning hardware into a bankable asset class"
"building the data science that makes hardware tech financeable"
discovered_patterns.description: Tangible focuses on creating curated/standardised datasets, playbooks and models specific to hard-tech assets (beginning with batteries). This suggests a strategy of building vertical data moats and domain-specific feature engineering to enable underwriters to assess novel collateral.
discovered_patterns.why_notable: Verticalizing ML and finance tooling for esoteric hardware assets is less crowded than horizontal fintech ML — this specialization can create defensibility through domain data and playbooks.

novel_approaches.approach: Agentic simulations / multi-agent interpretability chains applied to structured finance and asset-backed diligence
novel_approaches.why_novel: Agentic systems are often showcased in general automation tasks; applying them as financial simulations to de-risk hardware financings (e.g., modeling collateral performance, waterfalls, triggers) is unusual and domain-intensive.
novel_approaches.potential_impact: Enables automated, auditable diligence and risk modelling at scale for previously bespoke asset classes, lowering underwriting cost and time-to-deal.

novel_approaches.approach: Live reconciled canonical asset knowledge layer tailored to esoteric hardware assets
novel_approaches.why_novel: A live, reconciled canonical representation of complex hardware performance + financial states (receivables, collateral, covenants) is uncommon — most competitors rely on static reports or spreadsheets.
novel_approaches.potential_impact: Allows near-real-time covenant monitoring, automated funding triggers, and trustworthy AI outputs (reducing hallucinations) — critical for scaling asset-backed financing.

primary_models: Google Cloud AI (inferred: Vertex AI / Gemini family)

fine_tuning.fine_tuning_approach: No explicit evidence of fine-tuning. Privacy Notice only references using third‑party AI providers; no mention of in-house fine-tuning (LoRA/full) or hosted fine-tunes.

inference_optimization: cloud-hosted managed inference (using Anthropic / Google Cloud AI APIs)
no explicit evidence of quantization, distillation, local hosting, or custom inference stacks

model_routing.routing_strategy: Inferred multi-provider/task routing: privacy text lists multiple AI service providers, implying selection/routing across providers for different capabilities (confidence-based inference). No explicit function-calling or routing rules documented.

compound_ai.orchestration_pattern: Agentic orchestration (multi-agent workflows and simulations) with interpretability chains; agents likely use LLM APIs + tool calls to internal reconciliation/connector services and domain-specific code to produce auditable outputs.

sub_vertical: asset-backed / structured finance for hardware assets
sub_sub_vertical: hardware asset-backed financing for industrial equipment and energy storage assets

signals: ISO 27001 certified infrastructure, end-to-end encryption, and granular access controls
Security-focused language in privacy and data handling policies
AI-based features described and usage of third-party AI providers (Anthropic, Google Cloud AI)
Claims of live data infrastructure and single source of truth for transactions

team_analysis.founder_market_fit: Founders bring deep experience across hardware manufacturing, capital markets, product, and data science, aligning closely with Tangible's mission to build structured financing for hard-tech assets.
team_strengths: Cross-disciplinary leadership spanning hardware engineering, finance, product, and data science
Strong ML/data science talent with domain-specific applications (portfolio modelling, credit risk, AI for finance)
Global/dual-office setup (London and Lisbon) enabling distributed operations
Founders with direct exposure to hardware startups and capital markets, aligning incentives with customers
team_red_flags: Public track record of exits or large-scale prior successes not provided; small team size could impact scaling
Limited information on prior companies or investors beyond team roles; potential visibility gaps

success fee for companies ready to raise a facility
subscription fee for companies with live facilities

evidence: pricing tiers tied to stages in the transaction lifecycle implying direct sales engagement
Growth and Partnerships leader role suggesting partnership-driven GTM
market messaging focused on enterprise finance tooling and ‘plan your raise’ calls to action
onboarding, integration, and expert support features indicating a sales-assisted deployment

revenue_model.monetization_approach: Platform-plus-services model charging upfront readiness fees, success-based facility fees, and ongoing subscription for live facilities
unit_economics_signals: value proposition reduces manual work and speeds underwriting, suggesting scalable margins

collaborative diligence platform bringing lenders, borrowers, and experts onto one system

thought leadership and strategy messaging (hard asset super-cycle narrative)

quote from hedge fund manager about opportunity in hardtech
leadership focusing on growth and partnerships

stage_evidence: Pricing tiers announced: one-off fee, success fee, and subscription fee
Enterprise-grade security claims: ISO 27001, end-to-end encryption
Live data infrastructure and 'single source of truth' across assets
Comprehensive back-office finance toolkit: covenants, waterfalls, funding requests
AI-powered diligence layer and automatic data extraction

Collaborative diligence platform for transaction management

End-to-end integration of asset data with debt financing in a single platform
AI-powered diligence with pre-emptive data readiness and risk flags

Emphasis on automation, reporting, and risk management modules

Pricing terms are not fully detailed; adoption depends on lenders' willingness to use platform
Reliance on data migration and onboarding until integrations mature
Security claims are marketing-led; ensure ongoing certification and compliance
product_strengths: Real-time, reconciled data as backbone of transactions
Single platform for diligence, covenants, funding; reduces manual work
AI-powered automation for data extraction and risk flags
Structured finance expertise and lender collaboration support
Strong security posture (ISO 27001) with end-to-end encryption

unique_findings: They are explicitly building a live, reconciled data backbone that maps every asset and data point to a single source of truth for an asset-backed facility — implying an event-driven, streaming ingestion + canonical asset schema across heterogeneous on‑prem/cloud systems.

Focus on converting physical asset telemetry and performance models into 'financeable' inputs — i.e., bridging simulation/physics models with credit/cashflow models. This requires deterministic feature pipelines, provenance, and calibrated mappings from physical failure modes to expected cashflow impacts.

They combine agentic simulations and 'chains of interpretability' — suggesting a hybrid stack of simulation engines (domain models), ML predictors, and explainability layers that produce human‑readable justifications for valuation and covenant triggers, not just black‑box scores.

Heavy emphasis on operationalizing structured finance primitives (covenants, waterfalls, triggers, waivers) in a composable rules engine — likely formalized contract models with audit logs and deterministic execution, enabling lenders to run facilities off the platform.

Engineering tradeoff: they use third‑party LLM/AI providers (Anthropic, Google Cloud AI) while also promising 'no hallucinations, no made-up numbers' — this signals a strong RAG + grounded data architecture, numeric-verification layers, and probably model output validators/assertions tailored to financial/accounting invariants.

They position AI to do early diligence work: extract collateral details, reconcile receivables, and highlight red flags before manual review — implying robust OCR/ETL, entity resolution across docs/accounts, and automated anomaly detection tuned to structured finance risk signals.

Security-first deployment hygiene (ISO 27001, end-to-end encryption, granular access controls) combined with AI services means they've had to design secure connectors (VPC peering, private endpoints, or strict data gating) to let sensitive data be processed by third‑party models without compromising compliance.

Team composition is deliberately cross-disciplinary: computational physicists, agent/AI engineers, structured finance experts and product designers. That combination maps to a product that must translate domain simulations into regulatory‑grade artifacts (audit trails, explainable valuations).

story_angles.headline: Inside Tangible’s agentic capital-stack: orchestrating multi-agent workflows to automate structured finance
story_angles.summary: Tangible appears to be building an ‘agentic’ orchestration layer that turns static asset and financial data into machine-readable knowledge and automated deal workflows. Tell the technical story of how agentic models, connector-driven live data, and explainability chains are composed to replace manual diligence, covenant tracking, and waterfall calculations.
evidence: Team bios repeatedly reference 'agentic computing', 'agentic models', and 'agentic architectures' (Daniel Demmel, Simon Malzard).
Product descriptions: 'AI powered diligence layer for transactions', 'live data infrastructure', and 'the capital stack co-pilot'.
Mentions of 'chains of interpretability' and making 'static data into the machine-readable knowledge needed to scale hard‑tech'.

story_angles.headline: Turning batteries into bankable assets: Tangible’s vertical data moat and explainability-first models
story_angles.summary: Tangible is positioning proprietary, explainable asset-performance modelling as the core product advantage—standardising playbooks for esoteric assets (starting with batteries) so institutional capital can underwrite at scale. A data-first deep dive would examine their live-reconciliation pipeline, the physics-to-finance modelling stack, and how explainability chains translate device-level behaviour into underwritable credit inputs.
evidence: Privacy policy explicitly: 'creating the standardized playbooks for financing new esoteric assets - starting with batteries'.
Bernet Meijer bio: PhD in Computational Physics, 'turning complex equipment performance into explainable, financeable data for capital markets'.
Product messaging: 'Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time' and 'We translate what borrowers need into terms you can underwrite.'

story_angles.headline: They built structured-finance ops, not a lending marketplace — a contrarian bet on tooling over transactions
story_angles.summary: Rather than becoming a marketplace or pure lender, Tangible doubles down on the operational layer for transactions: live covenant/reporting engines, waterfall automation, and lender collaboration tools. Explore why a plumbing-first approach (tools that make structured finance tractable) could unlock far more capital than chasing transaction volume.
evidence: Product focus: 'Move your back-office tasks forward', 'The operational layer your facility can run off', 'Reporting without unwieldly spreadsheets'.
Go-to-market signals: pricing tiers for 'ready to raise a facility' and 'live facilities subscription fee' rather than pure loan origination.
Messaging to lenders: 'collaborative tools to assess and manage deals with less manual work, all from a single source of truth'.

story_angles.headline: From days to minutes: how AI reconciliation and automation promise near-instant diligence and reporting
story_angles.summary: Tangible claims to automate collateral extraction, receivables reconciliation, covenant monitoring and reporting — aggressive efficiency gains for hard‑tech CFOs who today operate on spreadsheets. A technical feature could measure the claimed automation pipeline, anti-hallucination safeguards, and the practical limits of 'minutes to a personalised roadmap'.
evidence: Product copy: 'Smart automation extracts collateral details, reconciles receivables, and highlights red flags, all before you start digging.'
Claims: 'Tangible to your data stack in minutes', 'Built with precision in mind, no hallucinations, no made-up numbers.'
Operational features: 'Manage covenants, triggers, waterfalls and funding requests' and 'near-instant onboarding'.

anti_patterns.description: Product risk of being primarily a thin orchestration layer on top of third‑party LLMs and cloud services instead of owning core proprietary capabilities.
evidence: Marketing emphasizes 'AI powered diligence layer' and 'Put our AI to work' but the public tech stack shows 'LLMs: []' and 'Approach: hybrid' with no listed proprietary models.
Privacy notice and vendor list call out AI Platforms and Cloud Computing Services as third‑party categories, implying reliance on external providers.
Many feature claims (reconciling, extraction, live data) can be built with managed LLMs + connectors, making a potential thin margin on orchestration.

anti_patterns.description: Several offerings read like a set of point features (covenant automation, reconciliation, AI extraction) that incumbents or large cloud providers could add to existing credit/treasury platforms.
evidence: Assertions such as 'AI powered diligence layer', 'Set the warnings that matter', and 'Reporting without unwieldly spreadsheets' describe discrete capabilities rather than a clearly differentiated, defensible product platform.

Language like 'The backbone of every transaction: live data infrastructure' and 'connect every asset and data point' suggests a single horizontal capability that could be integrated into larger suites.
Pricing snippets (one-off fee, success fee, subscription) imply modular offerings that are straightforward to replicate.

anti_patterns.description: Unclear sustainable competitive advantage: limited public evidence of unique proprietary data, exclusive partnerships, or patented IP that would create a defensible moat.
evidence: Competitive position listed as 'Moat: medium' and tech stack shows empty LLM list, reducing confidence in unique tech differentiators.
GitHub profile 'tangible' shows 0 public repos, suggesting little public codebase or community-driven assets to demonstrate technical lead or proprietary software.

Core claims (vertical data moats, live data ingestion, explainability chains) are plausible moats but the content lacks concrete examples of exclusive data sources, proprietary models, or long-term locked‑in contracts.

anti_patterns.description: Marketing uses strong claims ('fastest path', 'AI-first', 'agentic models', 'minutes to a personalised roadmap') without technical detail, benchmarks, or case studies to substantiate performance.
evidence: Phrases such as 'We are the fastest path to designing & delivering a capital structure you can scale with' and 'AI powered diligence layer for transactions' are marketing-forward but lack metrics, demo links, or client outcomes.

Repeated broad claims about transforming capital markets and creating standardized playbooks for esoteric assets (batteries) are not supported by concrete product details or customer references in the provided content.

Privacy policy contains strong-sounding security posture and data practices, but simultaneous assertions like 'We do not collect any information from third parties' conflict with other sections that list third-party vendors and Google Analytics.

anti_patterns.description: Positioning risks: offering overlaps with many incumbents in fintech, structured finance tooling, and data integration, making it hard to see a clearly unique positioning beyond the stated hard‑tech focus.
evidence: Core features (live reconciliation, covenant management, waterfalls, reporting) are common in structured finance and treasury platforms.
The product narrative emphasizes general benefits (fewer calls, faster decisions) that apply across many vendor solutions rather than a niche technical advantage unique to hardware finance.
Competitive and technical descriptions lean on common architectural patterns (connectors, data ingestion, agentic orchestration) without clarifying why Tangible's implementation is distinct.

competitors.name: Allvue Systems (private markets / structured finance platforms)
competitors.similarity: Provides portfolio, credit and structured finance management tools used by institutional investors and managers for asset-backed products and private credit.
competitors.how_different: Allvue targets asset managers and lenders with enterprise portfolio systems; Tangible targets hard‑tech founders + lenders with a borrower-facing capital‑stack co‑pilot, live asset reconciliation, AI diligence, and playbooks for esoteric assets (e.g., batteries). Tangible bundles borrower operational tooling, underwriting translation, and structured finance advisory rather than pure portfolio accounting.

competitors.name: nCino (bank operating system / loan origination)
competitors.similarity: Supports loan origination, servicing and workflow automation for banks and lenders.
competitors.how_different: nCino focuses on bank workflows and risk/origination for general lending; Tangible is vertical‑first (hard‑tech/hardware assets), emphasizes translating physical asset data into underwritable terms, real‑time collateral/receivables reconciliation, and seller/bidirectional collaboration between founders and institutional debt providers.

competitors.name: Finastra / FIS / Fiserv (traditional lending & asset finance software)
competitors.similarity: Large incumbents supplying loan servicing, asset finance, and structured finance software to banks and financial institutions.
competitors.how_different: Incumbents deliver broad, heavily customised enterprise installations. Tangible offers a fast onboarding, specialised playbooks for new/’esoteric’ asset classes, AI‑driven diligence, and a product focused on bridging founders to scalable debt rather than bank back‑office modernization.

competitors.name: Codat (data integration for lenders / accounting connectors)
competitors.similarity: Provides live, standardised access to accounting and business data to help lenders underwrite and monitor borrowers.
competitors.how_different: Codat is a data connectivity/integration layer. Tangible combines connectivity with domain models, asset‑level reconciliation, covenant & waterfall operationalization, AI extraction of collateral details, and structured finance templates — effectively a higher‑level product built on top of raw integrations.

competitors.name: CloudMargin / Traiana (collateral & margin management platforms)
competitors.similarity: Platforms focused on collateral, margining, and reconciliation for financial transactions.
competitors.how_different: Those products focus on collateral management for financial markets. Tangible focuses on physical/hard assets, receivables and project operations — mapping equipment performance to credit terms, managing covenants/triggers/waterfalls at the facility level and providing lender/borrower collaboration tools.

competitors.name: Securitize / Figure (asset tokenization & structured products fintechs)
competitors.similarity: Enable new ways to package and distribute assets to institutional capital, sometimes via tokenization or structured product engineering.
competitors.how_different: Tangible is not primarily a tokenization platform. It positions itself as the standardiser and operational layer for asset‑backed debt (starting with batteries) that helps lenders underwrite and manage facilities using live data and AI, rather than focusing on blockchain distribution.

competitors.name: Trade Ledger / Lendtech specialists (digital lending orchestration for asset finance)
competitors.similarity: Provide digital lending platforms/orchestration for asset finance and commercial lending workflows.
competitors.how_different: Trade Ledger is lender-focused and broad across asset types. Tangible is vertically specialised on hard‑tech/hardware, couples AI models that translate physical equipment performance into financeable metrics, and offers structured finance expertise + borrower operational tooling to run a live facility (covenants, waterfalls, funding requests) from day one.

differentiation.primary: Vertical focus on hard‑tech/hardware asset finance combined with an integrated product that translates physical asset and operational data into underwritable financial terms (capital‑stack design, covenants, waterfalls) using AI and structured finance expertise.
differentiation.technical: Live data infrastructure that reconciles every asset/data point in real time; AI‑powered diligence that extracts collateral details and reconciles receivables; agentic/data‑science models that turn complex equipment performance into explainable, financeable signals; ISO 27001 security and integrations into customers' data stacks for near‑instant onboarding.
differentiation.business: Go‑to‑market focused on bridging founders to institutional debt — offering one‑time advisory for raises, success fees, and subscription for live facilities — plus hands‑on structured finance experts to implement risk‑mitigating policies. They position as both product and advisory (software + capital markets know‑how) for a niche underserved by generic lenders or incumbents.
differentiation.positioning: Marketed as the 'capital‑stack co‑pilot' and the fastest path to a scalable, underwritable capital structure for hard‑tech companies — selling speed, standardised playbooks for new asset classes (batteries first), and reduced manual work vs spreadsheets/email chains.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: A combined stack of domain expertise in hard‑tech hardware + structured finance playbooks, live reconciled asset & receivable data, and proprietary AI/models that convert physical asset performance into explainable underwriting signals — plus human structured‑finance support to operationalise deals.
secret_sauce.defensibility: Moderate defensibility from (1) specialized domain knowledge and curated playbooks for esoteric assets (batteries/hard‑tech), (2) proprietary datasets and models produced from running real transactions (network/data effects), (3) integrations and live facility operations that create switching costs, and (4) institutional trust via security/compliance (ISO 27001) and relationships with lenders. However, large incumbents could replicate capabilities over time if Tangible does not accumulate unique data and lender network effects quickly.
evidence: “We’re creating the standardized playbooks for financing new esoteric assets - starting with batteries - so institutional investors can deploy capital with confidence, at scale.”
“Every asset and data point, reconciled in real time.”
“Put our AI to work — Smart automation extracts collateral details, reconciles receivables, and highlights red flags...”
“The capital stack co-pilot for ambitious hard-tech companies”
ISO 27001 certification and statements about end-to-end encryption and granular access controls (security as trust enabler).
Team composition blending hardware experience, structured finance market expertise and data/AI engineering (founders and principals described).

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Tangible's moat hinges on vertical focus, proprietary models mapping physical asset behaviour to credit metrics, and the network/data produced by operating live facilities. Those create meaningful early advantages: specialised playbooks for esoteric assets, lender relationships, and productised facility operations that increase switching costs. However, these advantages are replicable over time by incumbents or better‑funded fintechs if Tangible does not scale transaction volume, lock in lender integrations, and accumulate unique labelled datasets. Security & regulated trust help, but the company remains early stage (seed) so moat is not yet deep.

evidence_quotes: Put our AI to work Smart automation extracts collateral details, reconciles receivables, and highlights red flags, all before you start digging.
AI powered diligence layer for transactions
We offer products, features, or tools powered by artificial intelligence, machine learning, or similar technologies.
Use of AI Technologies We provide the AI Products through third-party service providers ('AI Service Providers'), including Anthropic and Google Cloud AI.
Our AI Products Our AI Products are designed for the following functions: - Machine learning models - AI automation - AI predictive analytics
The terms in this Privacy Notice govern your use of the AI Products within our Services.

Agentic simulations applied specifically to structured finance and deal de-risking — combining multi-step agent workflows with domain financial simulators to evaluate capital structures and counterparty risk.
Chains of interpretability that convert physics/asset-performance models into explainable, financeable features — bridging scientific simulation outputs to capital markets decisions.

Live, permissioned single-source-of-truth data layer that both grounds generative AI and drives transaction ops (covenants, waterfalls, funding requests), minimizing reconciliation drift between ML outputs and financial bookkeeping.

Multi-provider AI routing (Anthropic + Google Cloud AI) combined with a specialized micro-model topology for extraction, reconciliation, prediction, and compliance — an explicit multi-vendor model stack for redundancy and specialization.
